explicit_text: I moved to Tokyo when I was about 12 years old to work as an apprentice boy, since my family had become poor. In Tokyo, I worked at a tabi maker's home, then at a pastry shop, and later at Kimuraya bakery in Mita near Keio University. I lived in Tokyo through important events like the death of Emperor Meiji and the Great Kantō earthquake. Tokyo was where I spent my youth and early working years before eventually coming to America in 1920.
